questions.question: What is the primary goal of instruction scheduling in a compiler backend?
options: To minimize the total number of instructions emitted
To minimize pipeline stalls by reordering independent instructions
To select the cheapest machine instruction for each IR node
To reduce register pressure by spreading definitions across time
questions.explanation: Instruction scheduling reorders instructions within basic blocks to minimize pipeline stalls caused by data hazards (read-after-write dependencies), while preserving data-flow correctness.

questions.question: In tree-pattern matching for instruction selection, 'optimal tiling' uses dynamic programming to:
options: Find the shortest path through the control flow graph
Find the minimum-cost covering of the IR tree with machine instruction patterns
Determine the best register allocation for each variable
Schedule instructions in topological order
questions.explanation: Optimal tiling assigns a cost to each machine instruction pattern and uses dynamic programming to find the minimum-cost set of patterns that covers every node in the IR tree.

questions.question: In the RISC-V calling convention, the first 8 function arguments are passed in which registers?
options: t0–t6 and s0
a0–a7
x1–x8
ra, sp, gp, tp, t0–t3
questions.explanation: RISC-V passes the first 8 integer/pointer arguments in registers a0 through a7. Additional arguments are passed on the stack.

questions.question: In list scheduling, when multiple instructions are 'ready' (all dependencies satisfied), the scheduler typically picks:
options: The instruction with the fewest operands
The instruction with the lowest latency
The instruction on the critical path (longest dependency chain to the end)
The instruction that was written first in the original IR
questions.explanation: List scheduling prioritizes instructions on the critical path — the longest chain of dependent instructions from the current point to the end of the block. Scheduling these first minimizes total execution time.

questions.question: What is instruction scheduling, and why does it matter?
options: Ordering instructions alphabetically for readability
Reordering instructions to avoid pipeline stalls by keeping execution units busy
Selecting which CPU to run instructions on
Compressing instruction encodings
questions.explanation: Instruction scheduling reorders independent instructions to fill pipeline delays. If an instruction has a 3-cycle latency (e.g., load), the scheduler inserts useful work from later in the program between the load and its use, rather than leaving the pipeline idle.
